cve_desc: The binary MP4Box in Gpac 1.0.1 has a null pointer dereference vulnerability in the gf_isom_get_payt_count function in hint_track.c, which allows attackers to cause a denial of service.

finding.description: The pointer 'hmhd' is dereferenced (hmhd->type) before it is checked for NULL. If trak->Media->information->InfoHeader is NULL, accessing hmhd->type causes a null pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: hmhd is assigned from trak->Media->information->InfoHeader, which may be NULL.
hmhd->type is accessed without a prior NULL check, causing a null pointer dereference.

judgments.reasoning: The finding exactly matches the CVE: a null pointer dereference of hmhd before a NULL check. The fix adds '!hmhd ||' before the dereference, which is precisely what the diff shows.

actual_issue_summary: In GetHintFormat, hmhd (derived from InfoHeader) is dereferenced via hmhd->type before being checked for NULL, causing a null pointer dereference if InfoHeader is NULL. The fix adds a NULL check for hmhd before accessing hmhd->type.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. TensorFlow's type inference can cause a heap out of bounds read as the bounds checking is done in a `DCHECK` (which is a no-op during production). An attacker can control the `input_idx` variable such that `ix` would be larger than the number of values in `node_t.args`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. This is the only affected version.

finding.description: The bounds check on `ix` against `node_t.args_size()` is performed via DCHECK, which is compiled out in release/optimized builds. If `ix` (derived from `edge->src_output()`) is greater than or equal to `node_t.args_size()`, the subsequent call to `node_t.args(ix)` results in an out-of-bounds read on the protobuf repeated field.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function iterates over input edges and stores src_output() values in input_idx vector.
For each input node with experimental type set and type_id != TFT_UNSET, ix is read from input_idx[i].
DCHECK checks ix < node_t.args_size(), but this is a no-op in production builds.
node_t.args(ix) is called with out-of-bounds ix, causing heap out-of-bounds read.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-23592: the DCHECK bounds check on ix is a no-op in production, allowing an out-of-bounds read via node_t.args(ix). The CWE-125 matches as well.

actual_issue_summary: The bounds check on `ix` (input_idx) against `node_t.args_size()` used DCHECK which is disabled in production builds, allowing an attacker to trigger a heap out-of-bounds read via `node_t.args(ix)` when ix >= args_size. The fix replaces DCHECK with a proper runtime bounds check that logs a warning and returns early.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `GetInitOp` is vulnerable to a crash caused by dereferencing a null pointer. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: When the signature_def map contains `kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey`, the code looks up the same key in the `outputs()` map of that signature definition. The result of `find()` is never checked against `end()` before being dereferenced. If the outputs map does not contain `kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey`, the iterator returned by `find()` equals `end()`, and dereferencing it is undefined behavior (typically a null/invalid pointer dereference or out-of-bounds read).

step_by_step_execution.description: The function is called with a MetaGraphDef whose signature_def map contains kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey but whose outputs sub-map does not contain that key.
init_op_sig_it is found in sig_def_map, so the if branch is entered.
find(kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey) is called on the outputs map and returns end() since the key is missing.
The end() iterator is immediately dereferenced via ->second.name(), causing undefined behavior (null pointer dereference / crash).

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-23577: the unchecked find() result on the outputs map leads to dereferencing an end() iterator (null pointer dereference), which is precisely what the fix addresses.

actual_issue_summary: In GetInitOp, after finding kSavedModelInitOpSignatureKey in the signature_def map, the code calls find() on the outputs sub-map but never checks if the key exists there before dereferencing the iterator. If the outputs map lacks that key, dereferencing the end() iterator causes a null pointer dereference crash.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. When decoding a tensor from protobuf, a TensorFlow process can encounter cases where a `CHECK` assertion is invalidated based on user controlled arguments, if the tensors have an invalid `dtype` and 0 elements or an invalid shape. This allows attackers to cause denial of services in TensorFlow processes. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

code_snippets:
CASES_WITH_DEFAULT(proto.dtype(), p = Helper<T>::Decode(a, content, N),
                         dtype_error = true, dtype_error = true);

CASES_WITH_DEFAULT(proto.dtype(), p = FromProtoField<T>(a, proto, N),
                         dtype_error = true, dtype_error = true);
finding.description: Integer overflow in shape.num_elements(). If the TensorShape dimensions are individually valid but their product overflows int64_t, N can wrap to a small positive value. This causes a small buffer to be allocated (for N elements) while the stored shape_ implies a much larger tensor, leading to heap out-of-bounds reads/writes when the tensor is subsequently accessed using its shape dimensions.

code_snippets:
CASES_WITH_DEFAULT(proto.dtype(), p = Helper<T>::Decode(a, content, N),
                         dtype_error = true, dtype_error = true);

CASES_WITH_DEFAULT(proto.dtype(), p = FromProtoField<T>(a, proto, N),
                         dtype_error = true, dtype_error = true);

step_by_step_execution.description: Shape dimensions are individually valid but product overflows
Small buffer allocated

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2022-23571) is about an invalid dtype being passed through to set_dtype() when the tensor has 0 elements or incomplete shape (N <= 0), causing a CHECK/DCHECK assertion failure (CWE-617). The fix adds dtype validation in the else branch (when N is 0 or -1). The finding describes an integer overflow in num_elements(), which is a completely different issue unrelated to the actual vulnerability.

actual_issue_summary: When a tensor proto has an invalid dtype and 0 elements (N=0) or incomplete shape (N=-1), the code skips the dtype validation in the if-block (which only runs when N>0) and directly calls set_dtype() with the invalid value, triggering a CHECK/DCHECK assertion failure. The fix adds dtype validation in an else branch for the N<=0 case.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. When building an XLA compilation cache, if default settings are used, TensorFlow triggers a null pointer dereference. In the default scenario, all devices are allowed, so `flr->config_proto` is `nullptr`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: When compiler_for_platform fails with a status code other than NOT_FOUND, the error is silently ignored and execution continues past the error check. This means the function proceeds to use the platform and attempt to create a client even though compilation support is broken, potentially leading to undefined behavior or crashes downstream.

step_by_step_execution.description: compiler_for_platform returns an error with a code other than NOT_FOUND
The inner check only handles NOT_FOUND, so other errors fall through
Execution continues past the error block, potentially causing issues downstream

judgments.reasoning: This finding describes a different issue (incomplete error handling for compiler_for_platform) that is unrelated to the actual CVE. The CVE (CVE-2022-23595) is about a null pointer dereference when flr->config_proto() returns nullptr and is dereferenced without a null check. The fix adds a null check around flr->config_proto() usage. The finding does not mention this at all.

actual_issue_summary: In BuildXlaCompilationCache, flr->config_proto() can return nullptr (when default settings are used with no ConfigProto), but the code dereferences it unconditionally to access gpu_options().visible_device_list(). The fix wraps this access in a null check: if (flr->config_proto()) { ... }.

cve_desc: Glewlwyd 2.0.0, fixed in 2.6.1 is affected by an incorrect access control vulnerability. One user can attempt to log in as another user without its password.

code_snippets: if ((session_uid = get_session_id(config, request)) != NULL && user_session_update(config, session_uid, u_map_get_case(request->map_header, "user-agent"), issued_for, json_string_value(json_object_get(j_param, "username")), NULL, 1) != G_OK) {
finding.description: When password authentication fails (G_ERROR_UNAUTHORIZED), the code still calls user_session_update with the same arguments (including the final '1' flag) as the successful authentication path. Comparing the successful auth call: user_session_update(..., username, NULL, 1) with the failed auth call: user_session_update(..., username, NULL, 1) — they are identical. This means a failed login attempt on an existing session updates the session with the attacker-supplied username in the same way as a successful login, potentially allowing an attacker to associate an arbitrary username with their session without providing valid credentials.

code_snippets: if ((session_uid = get_session_id(config, request)) != NULL && user_session_update(config, session_uid, u_map_get_case(request->map_header, "user-agent"), issued_for, json_string_value(json_object_get(j_param, "username")), NULL, 1) != G_OK) {

step_by_step_execution.description: User input is read into variable `j_param`
Password authentication check fails with G_ERROR_UNAUTHORIZED
Despite auth failure, session_uid is retrieved and user_session_update is called with the attacker-supplied username and flag 1
The session is now updated/associated with the victim's username without valid credentials

state.value: session now linked to victim username

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the CVE-2021-45379 vulnerability: on failed password authentication, user_session_update is called with the attacker-supplied username, allowing a user to associate another user's identity with their session without providing valid credentials. The fix removes exactly this code block.

actual_issue_summary: When password authentication fails, the vulnerable code calls user_session_update() with the attacker-supplied username on the existing session, effectively allowing a user to update their session to be associated with another user's username without providing valid credentials. The fix removes this session update call from the authentication failure path.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. A malicious user can cause a use after free behavior when decoding PNG images. After `png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode)` gets called, the values of `decode.width` and `decode.height` are in an unspecified state. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: Integer overflow in the allocation size for the temporary uint16 buffer in the DT_FLOAT path. `height`, `width`, and `decode.channels` are all ints. The checks guarantee `width < (1<<27)`, `height < (1<<27)`, and `width*height < (1<<29)`, but do not account for multiplication by `decode.channels` (up to 4). The product `height * width * decode.channels` is computed in 32-bit int arithmetic and can overflow (e.g., (1<<29 - 1) * 4 > INT_MAX), resulting in a much smaller allocation than intended. `CommonFinishDecode` then writes the full image data into the undersized heap buffer, causing a heap buffer overflow.

step_by_step_execution.description: Large width/height values pass size checks
Multiplication overflows in 32-bit

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2022-23584) is a use-after-free: `png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode)` frees decode resources, then `OP_REQUIRES` accesses `decode.width` and `decode.height` in the error message. The fix removes the `CommonFreeDecode` call before the `OP_REQUIRES`. This finding describes an integer overflow in buffer allocation, which is an entirely different issue unrelated to the CVE.

actual_issue_summary: Use-after-free in DecodePng: `png::CommonFreeDecode(&decode)` is called to free decode resources, and then `OP_REQUIRES` accesses `decode.width` and `decode.height` (which are part of the freed decode context) in the error message string. The fix removes the `CommonFreeDecode` call before the `OP_REQUIRES`, letting `OP_REQUIRES` access the still-valid decode fields.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `AddManySparseToTensorsMap` is vulnerable to an integer overflow which results in a `CHECK`-fail when building new `TensorShape` objects (so, an assert failure based denial of service). We are missing some validation on the shapes of the input tensors as well as directly constructing a large `TensorShape` with user-provided dimensions. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: The overflow check uses an `int` (32-bit) variable `new_num_elements` to store the result of `MultiplyWithoutOverflow`, which returns `int64_t`. When the 64-bit result is positive but exceeds INT_MAX (e.g., 2^32), the truncation to `int` can wrap to a small non-negative value, bypassing the `< 0` overflow check. This allows construction of a TensorShape with very large dimensions, potentially leading to out-of-bounds memory access or other undefined behavior downstream.

step_by_step_execution.description: User provides input_shape with large dimension values that pass basic shape validation checks.
The overflow check loop computes new_num_elements using MultiplyWithoutOverflow, which returns int64_t, but stores the result in an int (32-bit).
MultiplyWithoutOverflow returns a valid positive int64_t value exceeding INT_MAX, which is truncated when assigned to the 32-bit int variable.

state.value: 0 or small positive value due to truncation

step_by_step_execution.description: The check `new_num_elements < 0` does not trigger because the truncated value is non-negative.
TensorShape is constructed directly with the user-provided large dimensions, causing a CHECK failure (assertion-based denial of service).

judgments.reasoning: This finding correctly identifies the core vulnerability: the faulty overflow check using a 32-bit int variable to store a 64-bit result from MultiplyWithoutOverflow, allowing the overflow check to be bypassed. The fix replaced this faulty check with TensorShape::BuildTensorShape which properly validates dimensions. This matches CVE-2022-23568 exactly.

actual_issue_summary: The overflow check in AddManySparseToTensorsMap used an `int` (32-bit) variable `new_num_elements` to accumulate the result of `MultiplyWithoutOverflow` (which returns int64_t), allowing integer overflow to go undetected. The TensorShape was then constructed directly from user-provided dimensions without proper validation. The fix replaces the faulty manual overflow check with `TensorShape::BuildTensorShape` which properly validates and returns an error status on overflow.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library. In version 2.11.1 and prior, if incoming RTCP XR message contain block, the data field is not checked against the received packet size, potentially resulting in an out-of-bound read access. This affects all users that use PJMEDIA and RTCP XR. A malicious actor can send a RTCP XR message with an invalid packet size.

finding.description: The while loop only checks that `rb_hdr` itself is within the packet bounds, but does not verify that the entire report block (of size `rb_len + 1` 32-bit words) fits within the packet. A crafted packet can have `rb_hdr` near the end of the buffer with a block type header indicating BT_STATS or BT_VOIP_METRICS, but with insufficient data remaining. After the loop, the code dereferences many fields of `rb_stats` or `rb_voip_mtc` (e.g., `rb_stats->jitter_min`, `rb_voip_mtc->jb_abs_max`) that reside past the end of the actual packet buffer, causing out-of-bounds reads.

step_by_step_execution.description: A malicious RTCP XR packet is received with a valid header but a block whose declared length extends beyond the actual packet size.

state.value: small value, e.g. just enough for XR header + block header
value from packet header, passes initial check

step_by_step_execution.description: The while loop iterates and rb_hdr points to a block header near the end of the buffer. rb_len is read from the block header.
The block type matches BT_STATS or BT_VOIP_METRICS, and the pointer is directly assigned without checking if the full structure fits in the packet.

state.value: set to rb_hdr, but the actual data extends beyond packet buffer

step_by_step_execution.description: After the loop, the code dereferences fields deep within the rb_stats/rb_voip_mtc structures, reading memory beyond the packet buffer.

state.value: out-of-bounds read from beyond allocated packet buffer

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-43845: the RTCP XR block data is not validated against the received packet size before being dereferenced, leading to out-of-bounds reads. The fix adds bounds checks for each block type, which is precisely what the finding identifies as missing.

actual_issue_summary: In pjmedia_rtcp_xr_rx_rtcp_xr(), when parsing RTCP XR report blocks, the code assigns block pointers (rb_rr_time, rb_dlrr, rb_stats, rb_voip_mtc) without verifying that the full structure fits within the received packet size. This allows out-of-bound reads when fields of these structures are later accessed. The fix adds bounds checks ensuring rb_hdr + sizeof(structure) <= pkt + size before assigning each pointer.

cve_desc: Depending on the way the format strings in the card label are crafted it's possible to leak kernel stack memory. There is also the possibility for DoS due to the v4l2loopback kernel module crashing when providing the card label on request (reproduce e.g. with many %s modifiers in a row).

finding.description: dev->card_label is used as the format string argument to snprintf. If an attacker can control the contents of dev->card_label (e.g., via an ioctl that sets the card label), they can inject format specifiers like %s, %n, %x, etc., leading to information disclosure, crashes, or arbitrary memory writes.

step_by_step_execution.description: An attacker sets dev->card_label to a string containing format specifiers (e.g., %s, %x, %n).

state.value: attacker-controlled string with format specifiers, e.g. "%s%s%s%s"

step_by_step_execution.description: vidioc_querycap is called, which calls snprintf with dev->card_label as the format string directly.
snprintf interprets the format specifiers, reading from or writing to stack memory, causing information leaks or crashes.

state.value: contains data read from kernel stack via format specifiers

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-2652: dev->card_label is passed as the format string to snprintf, enabling format string attacks (CWE-134). The fix adds "%s" as the explicit format specifier.

actual_issue_summary: dev->card_label was passed directly as the format string to snprintf, allowing crafted card labels with format specifiers to leak kernel stack memory or cause a DoS crash. The fix adds an explicit "%s" format specifier.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. Under certain scenarios, Grappler component of TensorFlow can trigger a null pointer dereference. There are 2 places where this can occur, for the same malicious alteration of a `SavedModel` file (fixing the first one would trigger the same dereference in the second place). First, during constant folding, the `GraphDef` might not have the required nodes for the binary operation. If a node is missing, the correposning `mul_*child` would be null, and the dereference in the subsequent line would be incorrect. We have a similar issue during `IsIdentityConsumingSwitch`. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: graph.GetNode() can return nullptr if the node referenced by tensor_id.node() is not found in the graph. The returned pointer is dereferenced without a null check, leading to a null pointer dereference.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function checks if the node is an Identity or IdentityNSingleInput with inputs.

state.value: an Identity node with at least one input

step_by_step_execution.description: ParseTensorName extracts tensor_id from node.input(0).
IsTensorIdControlling check passes (not a control dependency).
graph.GetNode() is called but the referenced node does not exist in the graph, returning nullptr.
input_node is dereferenced via IsSwitch(*input_node), causing a null pointer dereference.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches the CVE: in IsIdentityConsumingSwitch, graph.GetNode() can return nullptr and the result is dereferenced without a null check, which is precisely what the fix addresses.

actual_issue_summary: In IsIdentityConsumingSwitch (and a similar location during constant folding), graph.GetNode() can return nullptr when the referenced node is missing from the graph (e.g., via a maliciously altered SavedModel). The returned pointer is dereferenced without a null check, causing a null pointer dereference. The fix adds a nullptr check before dereferencing.

cve_desc: uWebSockets 18.11.0 and 18.12.0 has a stack-based buffer overflow in uWS::TopicTree::trimTree (called from uWS::TopicTree::unsubscribeAll). NOTE: the vendor's position is that this is "a minor issue or not even an issue at all" because the developer of an application (that uses uWebSockets) should not be allowing the large number of triggered topics to accumulate

finding.description: The buffer overflow check at the top of the function only drains when numTriggeredTopics is exactly 64 at function entry. However, within a single invocation of the function (and its recursive calls), numTriggeredTopics can be incremented multiple times without any bounds check — once per loop iteration for terminatingWildcardChild, once at the end for an exact match, and additionally within recursive calls via wildcardChild. This allows writing past the end of the triggeredTopics[64] array.

step_by_step_execution.description: publish() is called. The drain check at the top only fires if numTriggeredTopics is exactly 64 at entry.
The for-loop iterates over topic segments. Each iteration may increment numTriggeredTopics for terminatingWildcardChild and may recurse via wildcardChild, each of which can also increment.
After the loop, numTriggeredTopics is incremented again for the exact match, writing past the 64-element triggeredTopics array.

judgments.reasoning: This finding correctly identifies the CVE-2020-36406 vulnerability: the drain() check is only at function entry, so multiple increments within the loop and recursive calls can overflow the fixed-size triggeredTopics[64] array. The fix moves the drain check immediately before each increment point.

actual_issue_summary: The drain() call that prevents triggeredTopics buffer overflow was placed at function entry, only checking once. Multiple increments to numTriggeredTopics within the loop (for terminatingWildcardChild and exact matches) and recursive calls could exceed the 64-element array bounds. The fix moves the overflow check immediately before each triggeredTopics[numTriggeredTopics++] write.

cve_desc: A vulnerability was found in ImageMagick, causing an outside the range of representable values of type 'unsigned long' at coders/pcl.c, when crafted or untrusted input is processed. This leads to a negative impact to application availability or other problems related to undefined behavior.

finding.description: Off-by-one heap/stack buffer write: the byte is written to `command` via `*p++` unconditionally BEFORE the bounds check. When the buffer is full, the subsequent `*p='\'0'` writes one byte past the end of the `command` array.

step_by_step_execution.description: Bytes are read and written into command buffer

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2022-32546 is about an integer overflow (CWE-190) when casting a very large double value (e.g., 4e+26) to 'unsigned long' (size_t) at the lines `width=(size_t) floor(bounds.x2-bounds.x1+0.5)` and `height=(size_t) floor(...)`. The fix adds CastDoubleToLong() to safely handle this conversion. The finding describes a completely different issue — a potential off-by-one buffer write in the command parsing loop — which is unrelated to the CVE.

A crafted PCL file with extremely large bounding box values causes undefined behavior. The fix wraps the conversion with `CastDoubleToLong()` to safely clamp the value.

cve_desc: In Deark before v1.5.8, a specially crafted input file can cause a division by zero in (src/fmtutil.c) because of the value of pixelsize.

finding.description: Division by zero when bi->pixelsize is 0. The value of bi->pixelsize is read directly from the input file with no validation before being used as a divisor.

step_by_step_execution.description: bi->pixelsize is read from the input file via dbuf_getu16be(f, pos+18)
Division is performed using bi->pixelsize as divisor without checking for zero

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-28856: a division by zero in src/fmtutil.c due to the value of pixelsize being zero, which is precisely what the fix addresses by adding a check for bi->pixelsize>0.

cve_desc: The kernel in Amazon Web Services FreeRTOS before 10.4.3 has an integer overflow in queue.c for queue creation.

code_snippets: pxNewQueue = ( Queue_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( Queue_t ) + xQueueSizeInBytes );
finding.description: Integer overflow in the addition `sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes` when computing the allocation size for pvPortMalloc. The multiplication overflow is checked via configASSERT, but the subsequent addition of sizeof(Queue_t) is not checked. If xQueueSizeInBytes is large enough (close to SIZE_MAX), the addition wraps around to a small value, resulting in a small heap allocation. The queue then operates as if it has xQueueSizeInBytes bytes of storage, leading to a heap buffer overflow when items are enqueued.

code_snippets: pxNewQueue = ( Queue_t * ) pvPortMalloc( sizeof( Queue_t ) + xQueueSizeInBytes );

step_by_step_execution.description: xQueueGenericCreate is called with uxQueueLength and uxItemSize chosen so that their product is close to SIZE_MAX but passes the multiplication overflow check.
xQueueSizeInBytes is computed as uxQueueLength * uxItemSize. The multiplication overflow assert passes.
The addition sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes overflows, wrapping around to a small value. No check exists for this addition overflow.
pvPortMalloc allocates a small buffer based on the wrapped value.
The queue is initialized as if it has xQueueSizeInBytes bytes of storage, but the actual allocation is much smaller, leading to heap buffer overflow when items are enqueued.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-31571: the missing addition overflow check on `sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes` in queue creation. The fix adds precisely this check via configASSERT.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in `sizeof(Queue_t) + xQueueSizeInBytes` addition during queue creation in queue.c. The multiplication overflow was checked but the subsequent addition of sizeof(Queue_t) was not, allowing a wraparound to a small allocation size. The fix adds a configASSERT to check for this addition overflow.

cve_desc: Tensorflow is an Open Source Machine Learning Framework. The implementation of `SparseCountSparseOutput` is vulnerable to a heap overflow. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.8.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.7.1, TensorFlow 2.6.3, and TensorFlow 2.5.3, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: The variable `batch` is derived from a user-controlled int64_t index value but only checked against an upper bound (`batch >= num_batches`). There is no check that `batch >= 0`. If the sparse index has a negative value in dimension 0, `batch` will be negative, the upper-bound check will pass, and `per_batch_counts[batch]` will perform an out-of-bounds access on the underlying container (likely a std::vector).

batch is assigned from the negative index value
The check `batch >= num_batches` evaluates to false since negative < positive, so execution continues
per_batch_counts[batch] performs out-of-bounds vector access with negative index, causing heap overflow

judgments.reasoning: This finding identifies insufficient validation of the batch index derived from user-controlled sparse indices, leading to out-of-bounds heap access. This is a specific manifestation of the heap overflow vulnerability described in CVE-2022-21740, which is about insufficient input validation in SparseCount leading to heap overflow.

finding.description: Both `num_batches` and `num_values` are declared as `int` but assigned from `int64_t` / platform-dependent size values. If shape_vector(0) exceeds INT_MAX, the value is truncated, potentially resulting in a small or negative `num_batches`. This undermines all subsequent bounds checks that rely on `num_batches`.

step_by_step_execution.description: User provides a shape tensor with first element exceeding INT_MAX
num_batches is assigned from int64_t value truncated to int

state.value: negative or small value due to truncation

step_by_step_execution.description: Subsequent bounds checks using num_batches are ineffective

state.value: incorrect value undermines batch < num_batches check

judgments.reasoning: Integer truncation of num_batches from int64_t to int is another pathway to undermine bounds checks and enable the heap overflow described in CVE-2022-21740. The fix restructures the code entirely, removing the problematic shape_vector(0) to int assignment. This is a valid component of the overall insufficient validation that leads to heap overflow.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2022-21740 is a heap overflow in SparseCountSparseOutput caused by insufficient input validation. The `s > 0` guard is on the wrong side of the `&&` operator.

cve_desc: A Vulnerability of LG Electronic web OS TV Emulator could allow an attacker to escalate privileges and overwrite certain files. This vulnerability is due to wrong environment setting. An attacker could exploit this vulnerability through crafted configuration files and executable files.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions due to incomplete validation in `tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2`, an attacker can trigger undefined behavior via binding a reference to a null pointer or can access data outside the bounds of heap allocated arrays. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/84d053187cb80d975ef2b9684d4b61981bca0c41/tensorflow/core/kernels/quantize_op.cc#L59) has some validation but does not check that `min_range` and `max_range` both have the same non-zero number of elements. If `axis` is provided (i.e., not `-1`), then validation should check that it is a value in range for the rank of `input` tensor and then the lengths of `min_range` and `max_range` inputs match the `axis` dimension of the `input` tensor. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 6da6620efad397c85493b8f8667b821403516708. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

+++ fixed
@@ -5,7 +5,50 @@
 
     int num_slices = 1;
     if (axis_ > -1) {
+      OP_REQUIRES(
+          ctx, input.dims() > axis_,
+          errors::InvalidArgument(
+              "Axis is on a zero-based index, so its value must always be less "

+              axis_, " and input's dims was ", input.dims()));

+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_min_range.dims() == 1,

+                      "output tensors, but min_range is a 1-D tensor of size ",
+                      input_min_range.dim_size(0),
+                      " and input's axis dimension is of size ", num_slices));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_max_range.dims() == 1,
+                  errors::InvalidArgument(
+                      "If axis is specified, max_range must be a 1-D tensor "
+                      "whose size matches the axis dimension of the input and "
+                      "output tensors, but max_range dims are ",
+                      input_max_range.dims()));
+      OP_REQUIRES(ctx, input_max_range.dim_size(0) == num_slices,

finding.description: The number of elements in input_min_range and input_max_range tensors is never validated against num_slices (derived from input.dim_size(axis_)). An attacker can supply min/max range tensors with fewer elements than num_slices, causing out-of-bounds reads on min_ranges(i)/max_ranges(i) and out-of-bounds writes on output_min_tensor/output_max_tensor (which are allocated with the same shape as the min/max input).

num_slices is set to input.dim_size(axis_) without validating that min_range and max_range have matching sizes.
The loop iterates from 0 to num_slices-1, accessing min_ranges(i) and max_ranges(i) beyond their allocated bounds.

OOB read when i >= max_range size

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2021-37663: In tf.raw_ops.QuantizeV2, incomplete validation allows: (1) axis_ >= input.dims() causing undefined behavior, (2) min_range/max_range tensors with mismatched sizes vs. num_slices causing OOB access, and (3) empty min_range/max_range when axis=-1 causing null pointer dereference. finding.description: Out-of-bounds read when indices is a matrix with zero columns (e.g., shape [N, 0]). The IsMatrix check only verifies the tensor has 2 dimensions, not that the second dimension is >= 1. If indices has shape [N, 0] where N > 0, num_indices is 0, passing the emptiness check (since num_values would also need to be 0), but dim_size(0) is N > 0, so the loop executes and accesses column 0 which does not exist.

IsMatrix check passes since indices has 2 dimensions.
num_indices == 0 and num_values == 0, so the emptiness check passes.

cve_desc: Nginx NJS v0.7.2 was discovered to contain a segmentation violation in the function njs_value_own_enumerate at src/njs_value.c.

finding.description: Time-of-check to time-of-use (TOCTOU) vulnerability: `length` is obtained from `njs_object_length` early on, then `njs_value_to_integer` is called on user-supplied arguments (args[1] for `start`, args[2] for `delete`). If these arguments are objects with custom `valueOf`/`toString` methods, arbitrary JavaScript can execute during these conversions, potentially shrinking or modifying the array. However, `start` and `delete` were computed using the stale `length`. In the fast_array path, `array->start[n]` is then accessed where `n` can range up to `start + delete - 1`, which may exceed the array's actual (now-reduced) allocated size, causing an out-of-bounds read. The same issue applies to the second fast_array block where `array->length` may no longer reflect the true allocated capacity after side effects from the conversion calls.

step_by_step_execution.description: User input is read into variable `x`

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE is about uninitialized entries in the 'deleted' fast array when 'this' is a slow object with gaps. When njs_value_property_i64 returns not-found (not NJS_OK, not NJS_ERROR), the code skips setting deleted->start[i], leaving it uninitialized. This later causes a segfault in njs_value_own_enumerate when iterating the deleted array. The fix adds an else branch to call njs_set_invalid on those gap entries. The finding describes a completely different TOCTOU/side-effect issue with valueOf conversions and stale length, which is unrelated to the actual fix.

actual_issue_summary: When Array.prototype.splice() is called with a slow (non-fast-array) 'this' argument but the 'deleted' result array is a fast array, gaps in 'this' (properties that don't exist) cause deleted->start[i] entries to be left uninitialized. The fix adds an else branch to set those entries to invalid via njs_set_invalid(), preventing a segmentation fault when the uninitialized deleted array is later enumerated.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. PJSIP versions 2.12 and prior do not parse incoming RTCP feedback RPSI (Reference Picture Selection Indication) packet, but any app that directly uses pjmedia_rtcp_fb_parse_rpsi() will be affected. A patch is available in the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. There are currently no known workarounds.

finding.description: The attacker-controlled `padlen` value read from the packet is not validated against `rpsi_len`. If `padlen` is larger than `rpsi_len*8 - 16`, the unsigned subtraction `rpsi_len*8 - 16 - padlen` wraps around to a very large value. This huge value is then used to compute the string length passed to `pj_strset`, resulting in the `rpsi->rpsi` string referencing memory far beyond the actual buffer, enabling an out-of-bounds read.

pj_strset sets rpsi->rpsi to reference (rpsi_bit_len+7)/8 bytes, causing OOB read

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation of SVDF in TFLite is [vulnerable to a null pointer error](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/svdf.cc#L300-L313). Furthermore, because `GetVariableInput` calls [`GetMutableInput`](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/460e000de3a83278fb00b61a16d161b1964f15f4/tensorflow/lite/kernels/kernel_util.cc#L82-L90) which might return `nullptr`, the `tensor->is_variable` expression can also trigger a null pointer exception. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 5b048e87e4e55990dae6b547add4dae59f4e1c76. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

tensor->is_variable is dereferenced without null check, causing null pointer dereference

cve_desc: Out-of-bounds Read in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.7.0.

code_snippets: int res = r_charset_encode_str (ch, out, outlen, buf, len);
finding.description: Integer overflow when computing `outlen`. The variable `len` (of type `st64`) is validated to be at most `ST32_MAX` (2147483647), but `len * 4` can exceed `INT_MAX` (2147483647) when `len > 536870911`. Storing this result in `int outlen` causes signed integer overflow, yielding a negative or unexpectedly small value. This corrupted `outlen` is then passed as the output buffer size limit to `r_charset_encode_str`. If that function interprets the negative value as an unsigned large value, it could write beyond intended bounds; if it treats it as a small positive, the resulting `res` (and thus `len`) would be incorrect, leading to the main scanning loop processing uninitialized/zeroed buffer data with incorrect assumptions about content length.

The fix adds an upper bound check on needle (`needle < UT64_MAX - 4`), rewrites the comparison to `needle < to - 4`, and adds parentheses to ensure correct pointer arithmetic, preventing out-of-bounds reads.

Malicious inputs can cause the index to be out of range, leading to out-of-bounds heap access. The fix adds validation that group size matches output_strides size and that the computed index is within [0, out_flat.size()).

cve_desc: A memory leak in the rsi_send_beacon() function in drivers/net/wireless/rsi/rsi_91x_mgmt.c in the Linux kernel through 5.3.11 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by triggering rsi_prepare_beacon() failures, aka CID-d563131ef23c.

rsi_prepare_beacon() is called and fails, returning non-zero
Function returns -EINVAL without calling dev_kfree_skb(skb)

actual_issue_summary: Memory leak in rsi_send_beacon(): when rsi_prepare_beacon() fails, the allocated skb is not freed before returning -EINVAL, leading to memory consumption DoS.

cve_desc: OwnTone (aka owntone-server) through 28.1 has a use-after-free in net_bind() in misc.c.

finding.description: After freeaddrinfo(servinfo) is called, the pointer `ptr` (which points into the freed `servinfo` linked list) is dereferenced to access `ptr->ai_addr` and `ptr->ai_addrlen`. This is a use-after-free vulnerability.

step_by_step_execution.description: getaddrinfo allocates servinfo linked list; ptr iterates through it
Loop completes with ptr pointing to a successful node in servinfo

state.value: non-NULL, points to a node in servinfo

step_by_step_execution.description: freeaddrinfo(servinfo) frees the entire linked list including the node ptr points to
ptr->ai_addr and ptr->ai_addrlen are accessed in getsockname call - use after free
ptr->ai_addr is accessed again in net_port_get and net_address_get - further use after free

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-38383: use-after-free in net_bind() where ptr (into the freed servinfo list) is dereferenced after freeaddrinfo(). The fix replaces these accesses with a local naddr variable.

actual_issue_summary: After freeaddrinfo(servinfo) frees the addrinfo linked list, the code dereferences ptr->ai_addr and ptr->ai_addrlen (which point into freed memory) in getsockname(), net_port_get(), and net_address_get(). The fix uses a local union net_sockaddr variable instead.

cve_desc: Integer overflow in the VNC display driver in QEMU before 2.1.0 allows attachers to cause a denial of service (process crash) via a CLIENT_CUT_TEXT message, which triggers an infinite loop.

finding.description: Integer overflow in the VNC_MSG_CLIENT_CUT_TEXT handler. The expression `8 + dlen` where dlen is a uint32_t can wrap around. For example, if dlen is 0xFFFFFFF8, then `8 + dlen` equals 0, which is returned as int 0 (meaning 'message fully processed'). This causes the code to fall through and call `client_cut_text` with a massive length but a buffer that only contains 8 bytes of actual data, leading to a heap buffer over-read.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the integer overflow in `8 + dlen` in the CLIENT_CUT_TEXT handler, which is exactly the vulnerability described by CVE-2015-5239. The root cause is the same: a malicious client sends a CLIENT_CUT_TEXT message with a crafted large dlen value that causes integer overflow. The finding describes a buffer over-read consequence while the CVE mentions an infinite loop, but the underlying bug (unchecked large dlen causing integer overflow) is identical, and the fix (limiting dlen to 1MB) addresses exactly this.

actual_issue_summary: Integer overflow in the VNC CLIENT_CUT_TEXT message handler where `8 + dlen` (with dlen being a client-controlled uint32_t) can wrap around, leading to denial of service via infinite loop or other misbehavior. The fix adds a 1MB limit on the payload size.

cve_desc: TensorFlow is an end-to-end open source platform for machine learning. In affected versions the implementation for `tf.raw_ops.BoostedTreesCreateEnsemble` can result in a use after free error if an attacker supplies specially crafted arguments. The [implementation](https://github.com/tensorflow/tensorflow/blob/f24faa153ad31a4b51578f8181d3aaab77a1ddeb/tensorflow/core/kernels/boosted_trees/resource_ops.cc#L55) uses a reference counted resource and decrements the refcount if the initialization fails, as it should. However, when the code was written, the resource was represented as a naked pointer but later refactoring has changed it to be a smart pointer. Thus, when the pointer leaves the scope, a subsequent `free`-ing of the resource occurs, but this fails to take into account that the refcount has already reached 0, thus the resource has been already freed. During this double-free process, members of the resource object are accessed for cleanup but they are invalid as the entire resource has been freed. We have patched the issue in GitHub commit 5ecec9c6fbdbc6be03295685190a45e7eee726ab. The fix will be included in TensorFlow 2.6.0. We will also cherrypick this commit on TensorFlow 2.5.1, TensorFlow 2.4.3, and TensorFlow 2.3.4, as these are also affected and still in supported range.

finding.description: When CreateResource returns ALREADY_EXISTS, the raw pointer obtained from result.release() is never freed or Unref'd. The unique_ptr has already relinquished ownership via release(), and since ALREADY_EXISTS is silently ignored, the allocated BoostedTreesEnsembleResource is leaked. An attacker who can repeatedly trigger this code path (e.g., by calling the op when the resource already exists) can cause unbounded memory consumption.

step_by_step_execution.description: CreateResource is called with result.release(), transferring ownership of the raw pointer
CreateResource returns ALREADY_EXISTS status
The ALREADY_EXISTS case is silently ignored, leaking the resource

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2021-37652) is about a use-after-free / double-free that occurs when InitFromSerialized fails: result->Unref() frees the resource, but the unique_ptr still owns the pointer and will attempt to delete it again when leaving scope. The finding describes a completely different issue — a potential memory leak when CreateResource returns ALREADY_EXISTS — which is unrelated to the actual CVE.

actual_issue_summary: When InitFromSerialized fails, result->Unref() is called which frees the resource (refcount reaches 0). However, the unique_ptr still owns the pointer and will attempt to delete it when leaving scope, causing a use-after-free / double-free. The fix adds result.release() after Unref() to relinquish the unique_ptr's ownership of the already-freed resource.

cve_desc: In drivers/hid/hid-elo.c in the Linux kernel before 5.16.11, a memory leak exists for a certain hid_parse error condition.

finding.description: In the error paths (when hid_parse or hid_hw_start fails), the code frees `priv` but does not call `usb_put_dev(priv->usbdev)` to release the USB device reference acquired earlier via `usb_get_dev(udev)`. This leads to a reference count leak on the USB device, preventing it from ever being freed.

step_by_step_execution.description: elo_probe is called and priv is allocated via kzalloc
usb_get_dev(udev) increments the USB device refcount and stores it in priv->usbdev
hid_parse(hdev) is called and fails, jumping to err_free
err_free label: kfree(priv) frees the priv struct but usb_put_dev is never called, leaking the USB device reference

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-27950: the missing usb_put_dev() call in the error path causes a memory/resource leak. The fix adds usb_put_dev(udev) before kfree(priv) in the err_free label.

actual_issue_summary: In elo_probe(), when hid_parse() or hid_hw_start() fails, the error handling code frees priv but doesn't call usb_put_dev() to release the USB device reference obtained via usb_get_dev(), causing a reference count leak (memory leak).

cve_desc: A memory leak in the fastrpc_dma_buf_attach() function in drivers/misc/fastrpc.c in the Linux kernel before 5.3.9 allows attackers to cause a denial of service (memory consumption) by triggering dma_get_sgtable() failures, aka CID-fc739a058d99.

finding.description: When dma_get_sgtable fails, the function returns -EINVAL without freeing the previously allocated memory 'a', causing a memory leak.

dma_get_sgtable is called and fails, returning a negative value
The function returns -EINVAL without calling kfree(a), leaking the allocated memory

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2019-19069: a memory leak of 'a' when dma_get_sgtable fails in fastrpc_dma_buf_attach. The CWE (CWE-401) and description align perfectly with the CVE and the fix (adding kfree(a)).

actual_issue_summary: In fastrpc_dma_buf_attach(), when dma_get_sgtable() fails, the function returns -EINVAL without freeing the previously kzalloc'd memory 'a', causing a memory leak. The fix adds kfree(a) before the return.

cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.6.0.

finding.description: In the 'beach' error-handling label, the code unconditionally dereferences 'obj' via 'obj->cache_buf = NULL'. However, 'obj' is NULL when the jump to 'beach' occurs before 'obj' is allocated (e.g., when 'prelink_range' is NULL) or after 'obj' has been explicitly freed with R_FREE.

step_by_step_execution.description: load_buffer is called and obj is initialized to NULL
get_prelink_info_range_from_mach0 returns NULL, so prelink_range is NULL
The check '!prelink_range' is true, so execution jumps to 'beach' label
At the 'beach' label, obj->cache_buf = NULL dereferences the NULL pointer obj, causing a crash

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2022-0419: a NULL pointer dereference of 'obj' in the 'beach' error handler. The fix adds a NULL check before dereferencing obj, which is precisely what the diff shows.

actual_issue_summary: In the beach error-handling path of load_buffer, 'obj->cache_buf = NULL' unconditionally dereferences 'obj', which can be NULL if the goto beach occurs before obj is allocated (e.g., when prelink_range is NULL). The fix adds an 'if (obj)' guard around the dereference.

cve_desc: Buffer Over-read in GitHub repository bfabiszewski/libmobi prior to 0.11. This vulnerability is capable of arbitrary code execution.

finding.description: In the delete branch (mod == 'd', dir == '<'), `pos` is decremented without checking if it is already 0. This can cause `pos` to become negative, leading to out-of-bounds read via `decoded[pos]` and out-of-bounds write via `memmove(d, s, l)` where `d = decoded + pos` points before the buffer. The existing bounds check (`l < 0 || d + l > decoded + INDX_INFLBUF_SIZEMAX`) does not catch a negative `pos` because `l` becomes larger (compensating) and `d + l` can still be within the buffer upper bound.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is called with decoded buffer and rule bytes from corrupt input.
A rule byte causes dir='<' and mod='d' (delete mode, left direction).
In the else branch, dir=='<' so pos is decremented. If pos was 0, it becomes -1.
d = decoded + pos points before the decoded buffer. s = decoded + pos + 1 = decoded. l = *decoded_size - pos = *decoded_size + 1.
The boundary check `l < 0 || d + l > decoded + INDX_INFLBUF_SIZEMAX` does not catch this because l is positive and d + l = decoded + *decoded_size which is likely within bounds.
decoded[pos] is read out-of-bounds (before buffer start), and memmove writes to d which is before the buffer.

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the missing `pos < 0` check in the delete branch, which is exactly what the fix adds. The diff shows `pos < 0` being added to the boundary check in both the insert and delete branches. This matches the CVE description of buffer over-read due to wrong boundary checks in the inflections parser.

actual_issue_summary: The boundary checks in mobi_decode_infl failed to detect negative `pos` values in both the insert and delete branches. The fix removes the position check from the position-setting branch (c > 10 && c < 20) and instead adds `pos < 0` checks directly in the insert and delete operation branches. It also fixes the delete branch to check `s + l` (source end) instead of `d + l` (destination end) against the buffer maximum.

cve_desc: Out-of-bounds Read in r_bin_java_bootstrap_methods_attr_new function in GitHub repository radareorg/radare2 prior to 5.7.0. The bug causes the program reads data past the end 2f the intented buffer. Typically, this can allow attackers to read sensitive information from other memory locations or cause a crash. More details see [CWE-125: Out-of-bounds read](https://cwe.mitre.org/data/definitions/125.html).

finding.description: Out-of-bounds read when `sz` is smaller than 8 bytes. The code sets `offset = 6` and then reads a 2-byte unsigned short from `buffer` at that offset without first verifying that `sz >= offset + 2`. If the buffer is shorter than 8 bytes, this reads past the end of the buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: Function is called with a buffer of size sz < 8
offset is incremented by 6
attr is non-NULL, so we enter the if block
R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT reads 2 bytes from buffer at offset 6, but buffer has fewer than 8 bytes, causing out-of-bounds read

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches the CVE. The fix adds a check `if (offset + 8 > sz)` before the R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT read, which is precisely the missing bounds check identified by this finding.

actual_issue_summary: Missing bounds check in r_bin_java_bootstrap_methods_attr_new: when sz < offset + 8 (i.e., < 14 but effectively 8 given offset=6), the R_BIN_JAVA_USHORT macro reads 2 bytes past the end of the buffer. The fix adds a check `if (offset + 8 > sz)` before the read.

cve_desc: A buffer overflow vulnerability in pcx_write_rle() in contrib/japanese/gdev10v.c of Artifex Software GhostScript v9.50 allows a remote attacker to cause a denial of service via a crafted PDF file. This is fixed in v9.51.

finding.description: After `from += step`, `from` can be equal to or greater than `end`. The subsequent expression `data != *from` dereferences `from` before the `from == end` check due to short-circuit evaluation of `||`. This causes an out-of-bounds read whenever the pointer reaches or passes the end of the buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: The while loop iterates with `from < end`.
`data = *from` reads the current byte.
`from += step` advances the pointer, potentially making it >= end.
In the condition `data != *from || from == end`, `data != *from` is evaluated first due to short-circuit evaluation, dereferencing `from` which is now out of bounds.

judgments.reasoning: This finding correctly identifies the exact vulnerability fixed by the commit: the condition checks `data != *from` before verifying `from == end`, leading to an out-of-bounds access. The fix reorders to `from >= end || data != *from` so the bounds check occurs first. While the finding uses CWE-125 (out-of-bounds read) and the CVE uses CWE-787 (out-of-bounds write), the underlying bug is identical — the misordered condition in the if expression.

actual_issue_summary: In pcx_write_rle(), after advancing `from += step`, the condition `data != *from || from == end` dereferences `from` before checking if it has passed the end of the buffer, due to left-to-right short-circuit evaluation of `||`. The fix reorders the condition to `from >= end || data != *from` (also changing `==` to `>=`) so the bounds check happens first.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C. Versions 2.12 and prior contain a stack buffer overflow vulnerability that affects PJSUA2 users or users that call the API `pjmedia_sdp_print(), pjmedia_sdp_media_print()`. Applications that do not use PJSUA2 and do not directly call `pjmedia_sdp_print()` or `pjmedia_sdp_media_print()` should not be affected. A patch is available on the `master` branch of the `pjsip/pjproject` GitHub repository. There are currently no known workarounds.

finding.description: The initial buffer length check does not account for the total size of all format (fmt) entries. The check only validates space for media, transport, and a fixed overhead of 12+24 bytes, but the subsequent loop writes an arbitrary number of fmt strings into the buffer without any bounds checking, leading to a heap/stack buffer overflow.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function checks buffer length against media.slen + transport.slen + 12 + 24, which does not account for fmt entries.
The function writes the m= line header, media, port, transport into the buffer.
The loop iterates over fmt_count entries, writing each fmt string without checking remaining buffer space.
pj_memcpy writes fmt data past the end of the buffer, causing a stack buffer overflow.

judgments.reasoning: This finding directly identifies the CVE-2022-24764 vulnerability: the fmt loop writes data without bounds checking, causing a stack buffer overflow. The fix adds exactly this bounds check (end-p > m->desc.fmt[i].slen) before each write in the loop.

actual_issue_summary: In print_media_desc(), the loop writing format (fmt) entries and the subsequent \r\n did not check remaining buffer space, allowing writes past the buffer boundary. The fix adds bounds checks before writing each fmt entry and before writing the trailing \r\n.

cve_desc: NULL Pointer Dereference in GitHub repository mruby/mruby prior to 3.2.

finding.description: Integer overflow in `len+2` when `len` is a large positive `mrb_int` value. If `len` is close to `MRB_INT_MAX`, `len+2` overflows to a negative or small value, causing `mrb_stack_extend` to allocate a small (or no additional) stack buffer. The subsequent loop then writes `len` elements starting at `c->stbase+1`, resulting in a heap buffer overflow.

step_by_step_execution.description: len is a large mrb_int close to MRB_INT_MAX
len+2 overflows
mrb_stack_extend allocates insufficient space
Loop writes len elements past buffer

judgments.reasoning: The actual CVE-2022-0890 is a NULL Pointer Dereference (CWE-703). The fix addresses the case where 15+ arguments are passed to a fiber: they must be packed into an array (setting n=15) rather than placed individually on the stack. The finding describes a hypothetical integer overflow in `len+2`, which is not the vulnerability fixed by this commit. The real issue is that when len >= 15, arguments were not properly packed, leading to a NULL pointer dereference during VM execution.

actual_issue_summary: When a fiber is resumed/transferred with 15 or more arguments, they need to be packed into an array (with cibase->n set to 15 as a sentinel). The vulnerable code placed all arguments individually on the stack regardless of count, leading to a NULL pointer dereference when the VM later tried to interpret them. The fix packs 15+ arguments into an array and also reorders the `c->ci--` operation to occur before stack manipulation.

cve_desc: common/password.c in Pengutronix barebox through 2021.07.0 leaks timing information because strncmp is used during hash comparison.

finding.description: strncmp is used to compare binary hash digests. strncmp stops comparing at the first null byte ('\0'). If both the computed hash and the stored hash happen to share a null byte at the same early position, strncmp will return 0 (equal) without comparing the remaining bytes. An attacker can brute-force passwords whose derived hash starts with a null byte, dramatically reducing the search space and bypassing authentication. The correct function to use is memcmp, which compares all bytes regardless of null bytes.

step_by_step_execution.description: strncmp used on binary hash data stops at null bytes

state.value: binary hash potentially containing null bytes
stored hash potentially containing null bytes

judgments.reasoning: The CVE (CVE-2021-37848, CWE-200) is specifically about timing information leakage because strncmp is used for hash comparison, enabling timing side-channel attacks. The fix replaces strncmp with crypto_memneq (a constant-time comparison), not memcmp. The finding describes a different issue — null-byte truncation in strncmp (CWE-187) — which is a distinct vulnerability class with a different attack vector and impact. While both issues originate in the same lines of code, the finding does not describe the timing side-channel that is the actual CVE.

actual_issue_summary: The use of strncmp for comparing password hashes in check_passwd() leaks timing information, enabling timing side-channel attacks. The fix replaces strncmp with crypto_memneq(), a constant-time comparison function.

cve_desc: PJSIP is a free and open source multimedia communication library written in C language implementing standard based protocols such as SIP, SDP, RTP, STUN, TURN, and ICE. In affected versions if the incoming RTCP BYE message contains a reason's length, this declared length is not checked against the actual received packet size, potentially resulting in an out-of-bound read access. This issue affects all users that use PJMEDIA and RTCP. A malicious actor can send a RTCP BYE message with an invalid reason length. Users are advised to upgrade as soon as possible. There are no known workarounds.

finding.description: The reason length is read from the packet byte at offset 8 and clamped only against the destination buffer size, but never against the actual remaining packet size (size - 9). An attacker can craft a packet where the declared reason length exceeds the available data, causing pj_memcpy to read beyond the bounds of the packet buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: An RTCP BYE packet is received with size > 8, entering the if block.

state.value: e.g. 10 (only 1 byte of reason data available)

step_by_step_execution.description: reason.slen is set to PJ_MIN(buffer size, declared length at pkt+8). The declared length can be larger than actual remaining data.

state.value: e.g. 200 (declared by attacker in packet byte at offset 8)

step_by_step_execution.description: pj_memcpy copies reason.slen bytes from pkt+9, reading far beyond the actual packet buffer.

judgments.reasoning: This finding exactly matches CVE-2021-43804: the declared reason length in the RTCP BYE packet is not checked against the actual packet size, leading to an out-of-bound read. The fix adds exactly the missing check (reason.slen = PJ_MIN(reason.slen, size-9)).

actual_issue_summary: In parse_rtcp_bye, the reason length byte from the RTCP BYE packet was not validated against the actual received packet size, allowing an out-of-bounds read when the declared reason length exceeds the remaining packet data. The fix adds a clamp: reason.slen = PJ_MIN(reason.slen, size-9).

cve_desc: A double-free condition exists in contrib/shpsort.c of shapelib 1.5.0 and older releases. This issue may allow an attacker to cause a denial of service or have other unspecified impact via control over malloc.

finding.description: When the first realloc call fails (returns NULL) during the first iteration (i=0), `result` is also NULL, so the condition `!tmp && result` evaluates to false. The error is not handled, `result` remains NULL, and the code proceeds to dereference it via `result[i++] = dupstr(cptr)`, causing a NULL pointer dereference / write.

step_by_step_execution.description: First realloc fails when i=0 and result is NULL
Condition !tmp && result is false because result is NULL
Code falls through to result[i++] = dupstr(cptr) with result still NULL

judgments.reasoning: This describes a separate NULL pointer dereference bug, not the double-free vulnerability identified by CVE-2022-0699. The CVE and its fix specifically address the double-free of the `copy` pointer in the second realloc error path.

finding.description: In the error-handling path of the second realloc (after the for loop), `free(copy)` is called, but `copy` was already freed at the end of the for loop. This is a double-free vulnerability.

step_by_step_execution.description: copy is freed after the for loop completes
Second realloc fails, entering the error-handling path
free(copy) is called again, causing double-free

judgments.reasoning: This exactly matches CVE-2022-0699: the double-free of `copy` in the second realloc error path, which is precisely what the fix removes.

actual_issue_summary: In the `split` function, `copy` is freed after the for loop, but the error-handling path for the second realloc also calls `free(copy)`, resulting in a double-free. The fix removes the second `free(copy)` call.

cve_desc: A memory corruption issue was found in Artifex Ghostscript 9.50 and 9.52. Use of a non-standard PostScript operator can allow overriding of file access controls. The 'rsearch' calculation for the 'post' size resulted in a size that was too large, and could underflow to max uint32_t. This was fixed in commit 5d499272b95a6b890a1397e11d20937de000d31b.

finding.description: In the backward search case, the size calculated for the 'post' (suffix) string is incorrect. The formula `count + (size - 1)` does not correctly represent the number of bytes remaining after the match. For backward search, when a match is found, `count` equals the match position offset. The correct suffix length is `initial_count - count` (i.e., `r_size(op1) - size - count`), but the code computes `count + size - 1`. When `size > 1`, this yields a value larger than the actual remaining bytes, allowing subsequent reads past the end of the original string buffer.

step_by_step_execution.description: User input is read into variable `x`

judgments.reasoning: The finding correctly identifies the core issue in CVE-2020-15900: the 'rsearch' (backward search) calculation for the 'post' size uses `count + (size - 1)` which produces a value that is too large and can underflow to max uint32_t since `count` and `size` are unsigned. The fix replaces this with `op[-3].tas.rsize -= count + size` in the backward case. This matches the CVE description exactly.

actual_issue_summary: In the backward search (rsearch) path, the 'post' string size was calculated as `count + (size - 1)`, which is incorrect - it yields a size that is too large, and since `count` is a uint, can underflow to UINT32_MAX. The fix corrects the post size calculation and also fixes which operand receives the post string bytes (op[-3] instead of op1).

cve_desc: A flaw was found in the way the "flags" member of the new pipe buffer structure was lacking proper initialization in copy_page_to_iter_pipe and push_pipe functions in the Linux kernel and could thus contain stale values. An unprivileged local user could use this flaw to write to pages in the page cache backed by read only files and as such escalate their privileges on the system.

finding.description: The pipe buffer's `flags` field is never cleared when initializing a new buffer entry. If a previous use of this pipe buffer slot left `PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE` set in `buf->flags`, the flag persists. Because the page being inserted is a page-cache page (via `get_page`), a subsequent `pipe_write` operation will see `PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE` and append user-controlled data directly into the page cache page, overwriting file contents even without write permissions. This is the "Dirty Pipe" vulnerability (CVE-2022-0847).

step_by_step_execution.description: A new pipe_buffer is allocated in the while loop of push_pipe.

state.value: points to pipe->bufs[iter_head & p_mask], a potentially reused slot

step_by_step_execution.description: buf->ops, buf->page, buf->offset, and buf->len are initialized, but buf->flags is NOT initialized.

state.value: stale value from previous use of this pipe buffer slot, potentially containing PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE

step_by_step_execution.description: If buf->flags retains PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE from a prior splice or write, a subsequent pipe_write sees this flag and merges user data directly into the page-cache page, allowing writes to read-only files.

judgments.reasoning: This finding precisely identifies CVE-2022-0847 (Dirty Pipe): the missing initialization of buf->flags in push_pipe allows stale PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE to persist, enabling unprivileged writes to page-cache pages of read-only files. The CWEs (CWE-909 missing initialization, CWE-281 improper preservation of permissions) are appropriate.

actual_issue_summary: CVE-2022-0847 (Dirty Pipe): The `flags` field of newly allocated pipe_buffer structures in push_pipe() (and copy_page_to_iter_pipe()) was not initialized, allowing stale PIPE_BUF_FLAG_CAN_MERGE flags to persist from previous buffer uses. This enabled unprivileged users to write to page-cache pages of read-only files, escalating privileges. The fix adds `buf->flags = 0;`.

cve_desc: off-by-one in io_uring module.

finding.description: Off-by-one error in the bounds check: `fd > ctx->nr_user_files` should be `fd >= ctx->nr_user_files`. When `fd` equals `ctx->nr_user_files`, the check passes, but the index is out of range. `array_index_nospec` then clamps the value to 0, causing the code to operate on fixed file slot 0 instead of returning -EBADF. This allows a user to cancel I/O operations associated with the wrong file descriptor.

step_by_step_execution.description: The function is called with fd equal to ctx->nr_user_files.
The bounds check `fd > ctx->nr_user_files` is evaluated. Since fd == nr_user_files, the condition is false and execution continues.
array_index_nospec clamps fd since it equals nr_user_files (out of range), resulting in index 0.
io_fixed_file_slot is called with index 0, accessing slot 0 instead of returning -EBADF.
cd->file is set to the file from slot 0, and __io_async_cancel proceeds to cancel operations for the wrong file.
